title: Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) season 10

infobox.value: [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS")
No. in season | Title | Directed by | Written by | Original release date | Prod. code
1 | "Up the Rebels" | Don Weis | Robert Janes | September 15, 1977 (1977-09-15) | 1310-1729-0701
2 | "You Don't See Many Pirates These Days" | Ronald Satlof | *S* : James Lydon *T* : Bill Stratton | September 22, 1977 (1977-09-22) | 1310-1729-0708
3 | "The Cop on the Cover" | Paul Stanley | *T* : [Gerry Day](/wiki/Gerry_Day "Gerry Day") *S/T* : Anne Collins | September 29, 1977 (1977-09-29) | 1310-1729-0704

infobox.key: When a magazine reporter ([Jean Simmons](/wiki/Jean_Simmons "Jean Simmons")) writes a story about Five-O, McGarrett tries to discreetly locate two missing children.

infobox.value: 4 | "The Friends of Joey Kalima" | Douglas Green and Don Weis | Robert Janes | October 13, 1977 (1977-10-13) | 1310-1729-0706
5 | "The Descent of the Torches" | Douglas Green | Alvin Sapinsley | October 20, 1977 (1977-10-20) | 1310-1729-0712

infobox.key: A mysterious intruder kills two members of an archaeological dig that is attempting to find a king's burial site. [George DiCenzo](/wiki/George_DiCenzo "George DiCenzo"), [Geraldine Page](/wiki/Geraldine_Page "Geraldine Page") and [Lou Richards](/wiki/Lou_Richards_(actor) "Lou Richards (actor)") guest stars.

infobox.value: 6 | "The Ninth Step" | Dennis Donnelly | Robert Pirosh | October 27, 1977 (1977-10-27) | 1310-1729-0710

infobox.key: A recovering alcoholic ex-cop ([Gil Gerard](/wiki/Gil_Gerard "Gil Gerard")) puts his life in danger after he returns to Hawaii to make amends to McGarrett. [Emilio Delgado](/wiki/Emilio_Delgado "Emilio Delgado") guest stars.

infobox.value: 7 | "Shake Hands with the Man on the Moon" | Ronald Satlof | *S* : Diana Kopald Marcus *S/T* : Robert Janes | November 10, 1977 (1977-11-10) | 1310-1729-0714

infobox.key: A reporter investigating a sleazy real estate developer is murdered, but the developer has an air-tight alibi. [Allan Miller](/wiki/Allan_Miller "Allan Miller") and [Christina Hart](/wiki/Christina_Hart "Christina Hart") guest stars.

infobox.value: 8 | "Deadly Doubles" | Marc Daniels | Robert Janes | November 17, 1977 (1977-11-17) | 1310-1729-0705
9 | "Deep Cover" | Steven H. Stern | Robert Janes | December 8, 1977 (1977-12-08) | 1310-1729-0717
10 | "Tsunami" | Harvey S. Laidman | Don Balluck | December 22, 1977 (1977-12-22) | 1310-1729-0707

infobox.key: A group of students create a fake tsunami so that they can rob a bank after the city is evacuated. [Leigh McCloskey](/wiki/Leigh_McCloskey "Leigh McCloskey"), [Ayn Ruymen](/wiki/Ayn_Ruymen "Ayn Ruymen"), [Sidney Clute](/wiki/Sidney_Clute "Sidney Clute") and [Lyle Bettger](/wiki/Lyle_Bettger "Lyle Bettger") guest stars.

infobox.value: 11 | "East Wind, Ill Wind" | Reza Badiyi | Edwin Blum | December 29, 1977 (1977-12-29) | 1310-1729-0703
12 | "Tread the King's Shadow" | Reza Badiyi | Harold Swanton | January 5, 1978 (1978-01-05) | 1310-1729-0713

infobox.key: The Governor asks a reluctant McGarrett to investigate the kidnapping of a racist shipping-magnate's daughter. [James Sikking](/wiki/James_Sikking "James Sikking") and [John Marley](/wiki/John_Marley "John Marley") guest stars.

infobox.value: 13 | "The Big Aloha" | Marc Daniels | Gerry Day | January 12, 1978 (1978-01-12) | 1310-1729-0716

infobox.key: A physician is shot to death on a remote island and a young doctor from a local clinic is the prime suspect. [Eleanor Parker](/wiki/Eleanor_Parker "Eleanor Parker"), [Lara Parker](/wiki/Lara_Parker "Lara Parker"), Cal Bellini and [John Reilly](/wiki/John_Reilly_(actor,_born_1934) "John Reilly (actor, born 1934)") guest stars.

infobox.value: 14 | "A Short Walk on the Longshore" | Don Weis | Richard DeLong Adams | February 2, 1978 (1978-02-02) | 1310-1729-0720

infobox.key: A candidate to head a longshoreman's union is murdered in broad daylight, yet there are few clues about the crime. With few leads, McGarrett decides to locate the girlfriend of the murdered candidate, whom he believes has vital information about the crime, by going undercover. [Sharon Farrell](/wiki/Sharon_Farrell "Sharon Farrell"), [Michael Conrad](/wiki/Michael_Conrad "Michael Conrad") and [Milton Selzer](/wiki/Milton_Selzer "Milton Selzer") guest stars.

infobox.value: 15 | "The Silk Trap" | Dennis Donnelly | Robert Janes | February 9, 1978 (1978-02-09) | 1310-1729-0726
16 | "Head to Head" | Jack Whitman | Leonard B. Kaufman | February 16, 1978 (1978-02-16) | 1310-1729-0725

infobox.key: McGarrett teams with an FBI agent after the syndicate sends a hit man to kill an organized-crime informant. [Charles Cioffi](/wiki/Charles_Cioffi "Charles Cioffi") and [George Grizzard](/wiki/George_Grizzard "George Grizzard") guest stars.

infobox.value: 17 | "Tall on the Wave" | Ronald Satlof | Bill Stratton | March 2, 1978 (1978-03-02) | 1310-1729-0711
18 | "Angel in Blue" | Allen Reisner | Irv Pearlberg | March 9, 1978 (1978-03-09) | 1310-1729-0702

infobox.key: After one of Five-O's undercover men is shot in the back, McGarrett enlists a Maui policewoman's help. [Vic Tayback](/wiki/Vic_Tayback "Vic Tayback"), [Carol Lynley](/wiki/Carol_Lynley "Carol Lynley") and [Enrique Novi](/wiki/Enrique_Novi "Enrique Novi") guest stars.

infobox.value: 19 | "When Does a War End?" | Ernest Pintoff | Arthur Bernard Lewis | March 16, 1978 (1978-03-16) | 1310-1729-0719

infobox.key: A young man ([David Dukes](/wiki/David_Dukes "David Dukes")) seeks revenge on a Japanese American businessman who once tortured his father in a Japanese prison camp.

infobox.value: 20 | "Invitation to Murder" | Harry Harris | Seeleg Lester | March 23, 1978 (1978-03-23) | 1310-1729-0718

infobox.key: The heirs to a wealthy murdered artist are now falling victim to lethal booby traps set in their prized possessions. [Christina Kokubo](/wiki/Christina_Kokubo "Christina Kokubo"), [Anthony Caruso](/wiki/Anthony_Caruso_(actor) "Anthony Caruso (actor)"), [Eduard Franz](/wiki/Eduard_Franz "Eduard Franz") and [Lyle Bettger](/wiki/Lyle_Bettger "Lyle Bettger") guest stars.

infobox.value: 21 | "Frozen Assets" | Reza Badiyi | Seeleg Lester & Sam Neuman | March 30, 1978 (1978-03-30) | 1310-1729-0724
22 | "My Friend, the Enemy" | Noel Black | Gerry Day | April 13, 1978 (1978-04-13) | 1310-1729-0721

infobox.key: A reporter ([Luciana Paluzzi](/wiki/Luciana_Paluzzi "Luciana Paluzzi")) from an Italian magazine unearths a plot to kidnap a visiting Middle Eastern princess. (Similar to episode "The Cop on The Cover")

infobox.value: 23 | "A Stranger in His Grave" | Richard Benedict | Arthur Bernard Lewis | April 27, 1978 (1978-04-27) | 1310-1729-0722

[Season 9](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_9 "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) season 9")  **Next** → [Season 11](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_11 "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) season 11") | |

This was Boyd's final role; he died three months before the episode's first airing. Final filmed appearance of recurring character Che Fong ([Harry Endo](/wiki/Harry_Endo "Harry Endo")). | | | | | | |

| Season chronology | |
| ← **Previous** [Season 9](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_9 "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) season 9")  **Next** → [Season 11](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_11 "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) season 11") | |

This is a list of episodes from the tenth season of *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*. This season marks the final series appearance of [Kam Fong Chun](/wiki/Kam_Fong_Chun "Kam Fong Chun").

## Broadcast history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_10&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Broadcast history")]

The season originally aired Thursdays at 9:00-10:00 pm (EST).[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## DVD release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_10&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: DVD release")]

The season was released on DVD by Paramount Home Video.

## Episodes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_10&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Episodes")]

See also: [List of Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) episodes](/wiki/List_of_Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_episodes "List of Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) episodes")

| No. overall | No. in season | Title | Directed by | Written by | Original release date | Prod. code |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 218 | 1 | "Up the Rebels" | Don Weis | Robert Janes | September 15, 1977 (1977-09-15) | 1310-1729-0701 |
| An Irish terrorist ([Stephen Boyd](/wiki/Stephen_Boyd "Stephen Boyd")) poses as a priest in order to buy bombs and weapons in Hawaii for use in his home country. **NOTE:** This was Boyd's final role; he died three months before the episode's first airing. Final filmed appearance of recurring character Che Fong ([Harry Endo](/wiki/Harry_Endo "Harry Endo")). | | | | | | |
| 219 | 2 | "You Don't See Many Pirates These Days" | Ronald Satlof | *S* : James Lydon *T* : Bill Stratton | September 22, 1977 (1977-09-22) | 1310-1729-0708 |
| McGarrett teams with a Navy commander ([Mark Lenard](/wiki/Mark_Lenard "Mark Lenard")) to search for a cargo ship hijacked by armed and dangerous men. **NOTE:** First appearance of Charlie, the forensic laboratory assistant (here played by Josie Over). | | | | | | |
| 220 | 3 | "The Cop on the Cover" | Paul Stanley | *T* : [Gerry Day](/wiki/Gerry_Day "Gerry Day") *S/T* : Anne Collins | September 29, 1977 (1977-09-29) | 1310-1729-0704 |
| When a magazine reporter ([Jean Simmons](/wiki/Jean_Simmons "Jean Simmons")) writes a story about Five-O, McGarrett tries to discreetly locate two missing children. | | | | | | |
| 221 | 4 | "The Friends of Joey Kalima" | Douglas Green and Don Weis | Robert Janes | October 13, 1977 (1977-10-13) | 1310-1729-0706 |
| Rookie cop Joey Kalima ([John Rubinstein](/wiki/John_Rubinstein "John Rubinstein")) is framed for departmental corruption by a group attempting to rob a bank. [Alan Oppenheimer](/wiki/Alan_Oppenheimer "Alan Oppenheimer") and [Elaine Giftos](/wiki/Elaine_Giftos "Elaine Giftos") guest stars. **NOTE:** Charlie, the forensic laboratory assistant, is played by Lydia Lei Kayahara. | | | | | | |
| 222 | 5 | "The Descent of the Torches" | Douglas Green | Alvin Sapinsley | October 20, 1977 (1977-10-20) | 1310-1729-0712 |
| A mysterious intruder kills two members of an archaeological dig that is attempting to find a king's burial site. [George DiCenzo](/wiki/George_DiCenzo "George DiCenzo"), [Geraldine Page](/wiki/Geraldine_Page "Geraldine Page") and [Lou Richards](/wiki/Lou_Richards_(actor) "Lou Richards (actor)") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 223 | 6 | "The Ninth Step" | Dennis Donnelly | Robert Pirosh | October 27, 1977 (1977-10-27) | 1310-1729-0710 |
| A recovering alcoholic ex-cop ([Gil Gerard](/wiki/Gil_Gerard "Gil Gerard")) puts his life in danger after he returns to Hawaii to make amends to McGarrett. [Emilio Delgado](/wiki/Emilio_Delgado "Emilio Delgado") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 224 | 7 | "Shake Hands with the Man on the Moon" | Ronald Satlof | *S* : Diana Kopald Marcus *S/T* : Robert Janes | November 10, 1977 (1977-11-10) | 1310-1729-0714 |
| A reporter investigating a sleazy real estate developer is murdered, but the developer has an air-tight alibi. [Allan Miller](/wiki/Allan_Miller "Allan Miller") and [Christina Hart](/wiki/Christina_Hart "Christina Hart") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 225 | 8 | "Deadly Doubles" | Marc Daniels | Robert Janes | November 17, 1977 (1977-11-17) | 1310-1729-0705 |
| A Soviet tennis star is caught up in a murder after she decides to defect to be with her American boyfriend. [Kurt Russell](/wiki/Kurt_Russell "Kurt Russell"), [Tim Matheson](/wiki/Tim_Matheson "Tim Matheson") and [Stefan Gierasch](/wiki/Stefan_Gierasch "Stefan Gierasch") guest stars. **NOTE:** Charlie, the forensic laboratory assistant, is played by Lydia Lei Kayahara. | | | | | | |
| 226 | 9 | "Deep Cover" | Steven H. Stern | Robert Janes | December 8, 1977 (1977-12-08) | 1310-1729-0717 |
| A Soviet spy ring led by a *[femme fatale](/wiki/Femme_fatale "Femme fatale")* kidnaps an engineer on a nuclear sub and steals classified data. [Geoffrey Lewis](/wiki/Geoffrey_Lewis_(actor) "Geoffrey Lewis (actor)") and [Maud Adams](/wiki/Maud_Adams "Maud Adams") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 227 | 10 | "Tsunami" | Harvey S. Laidman | Don Balluck | December 22, 1977 (1977-12-22) | 1310-1729-0707 |
| A group of students create a fake tsunami so that they can rob a bank after the city is evacuated. [Leigh McCloskey](/wiki/Leigh_McCloskey "Leigh McCloskey"), [Ayn Ruymen](/wiki/Ayn_Ruymen "Ayn Ruymen"), [Sidney Clute](/wiki/Sidney_Clute "Sidney Clute") and [Lyle Bettger](/wiki/Lyle_Bettger "Lyle Bettger") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 228 | 11 | "East Wind, Ill Wind" | Reza Badiyi | Edwin Blum | December 29, 1977 (1977-12-29) | 1310-1729-0703 |
| After an Asian diplomat drowns in a pool, his wife is convinced forces from their home country are responsible. [Sian Barbara Allen](/wiki/Sian_Barbara_Allen "Sian Barbara Allen"), [Marisa Pavan](/wiki/Marisa_Pavan "Marisa Pavan"), [Michael Durrell](/wiki/Michael_Durrell "Michael Durrell") and [Bill Edwards](/wiki/Bill_Edwards_(actor) "Bill Edwards (actor)") guest stars. **NOTE:** Final series appearance of Bill Edwards as the character Jonathan Kaye; [Lyle Bettger](/wiki/Lyle_Bettger "Lyle Bettger") would take over the role in Episode 15. | | | | | | |
| 229 | 12 | "Tread the King's Shadow" | Reza Badiyi | Harold Swanton | January 5, 1978 (1978-01-05) | 1310-1729-0713 |
| The Governor asks a reluctant McGarrett to investigate the kidnapping of a racist shipping-magnate's daughter. [James Sikking](/wiki/James_Sikking "James Sikking") and [John Marley](/wiki/John_Marley "John Marley") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 230 | 13 | "The Big Aloha" | Marc Daniels | Gerry Day | January 12, 1978 (1978-01-12) | 1310-1729-0716 |
| A physician is shot to death on a remote island and a young doctor from a local clinic is the prime suspect. [Eleanor Parker](/wiki/Eleanor_Parker "Eleanor Parker"), [Lara Parker](/wiki/Lara_Parker "Lara Parker"), Cal Bellini and [John Reilly](/wiki/John_Reilly_(actor,_born_1934) "John Reilly (actor, born 1934)") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 231 | 14 | "A Short Walk on the Longshore" | Don Weis | Richard DeLong Adams | February 2, 1978 (1978-02-02) | 1310-1729-0720 |
| A candidate to head a longshoreman's union is murdered in broad daylight, yet there are few clues about the crime. With few leads, McGarrett decides to locate the girlfriend of the murdered candidate, whom he believes has vital information about the crime, by going undercover. [Sharon Farrell](/wiki/Sharon_Farrell "Sharon Farrell"), [Michael Conrad](/wiki/Michael_Conrad "Michael Conrad") and [Milton Selzer](/wiki/Milton_Selzer "Milton Selzer") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 232 | 15 | "The Silk Trap" | Dennis Donnelly | Robert Janes | February 9, 1978 (1978-02-09) | 1310-1729-0726 |
| A congressman ([David Birney](/wiki/David_Birney "David Birney")) cheats on his wife while judging a beauty contest, and is blackmailed by a dangerous man ([Soon-Tek Oh](/wiki/Soon-Tek_Oh "Soon-Tek Oh")). **NOTE:** This was Kam Fong's final episode to be filmed. [Lyle Bettger](/wiki/Lyle_Bettger "Lyle Bettger") takes over the role of Jonathan Kaye. | | | | | | |
| 233 | 16 | "Head to Head" | Jack Whitman | Leonard B. Kaufman | February 16, 1978 (1978-02-16) | 1310-1729-0725 |
| McGarrett teams with an FBI agent after the syndicate sends a hit man to kill an organized-crime informant. [Charles Cioffi](/wiki/Charles_Cioffi "Charles Cioffi") and [George Grizzard](/wiki/George_Grizzard "George Grizzard") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 234 | 17 | "Tall on the Wave" | Ronald Satlof | Bill Stratton | March 2, 1978 (1978-03-02) | 1310-1729-0711 |
| When judging a surfing contest, Danny investigates the murder of a competitor's two-timing girlfriend. [Lisa Eilbacher](/wiki/Lisa_Eilbacher "Lisa Eilbacher") guest star. **NOTE:** Final appearance of Charlie, the forensic laboratory assistant (here played by Lydia Lei Kayahara). | | | | | | |
| 235 | 18 | "Angel in Blue" | Allen Reisner | Irv Pearlberg | March 9, 1978 (1978-03-09) | 1310-1729-0702 |
| After one of Five-O's undercover men is shot in the back, McGarrett enlists a Maui policewoman's help. [Vic Tayback](/wiki/Vic_Tayback "Vic Tayback"), [Carol Lynley](/wiki/Carol_Lynley "Carol Lynley") and [Enrique Novi](/wiki/Enrique_Novi "Enrique Novi") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 236 | 19 | "When Does a War End?" | Ernest Pintoff | Arthur Bernard Lewis | March 16, 1978 (1978-03-16) | 1310-1729-0719 |
| A young man ([David Dukes](/wiki/David_Dukes "David Dukes")) seeks revenge on a Japanese American businessman who once tortured his father in a Japanese prison camp. | | | | | | |
| 237 | 20 | "Invitation to Murder" | Harry Harris | Seeleg Lester | March 23, 1978 (1978-03-23) | 1310-1729-0718 |
| The heirs to a wealthy murdered artist are now falling victim to lethal booby traps set in their prized possessions. [Christina Kokubo](/wiki/Christina_Kokubo "Christina Kokubo"), [Anthony Caruso](/wiki/Anthony_Caruso_(actor) "Anthony Caruso (actor)"), [Eduard Franz](/wiki/Eduard_Franz "Eduard Franz") and [Lyle Bettger](/wiki/Lyle_Bettger "Lyle Bettger") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 238 | 21 | "Frozen Assets" | Reza Badiyi | Seeleg Lester & Sam Neuman | March 30, 1978 (1978-03-30) | 1310-1729-0724 |
| Five-O is summoned after a mystery writer ([Mildred Natwick](/wiki/Mildred_Natwick "Mildred Natwick")) investigates a fraudulent, and murderous, cryogenics outfit. [Peter Lawford](/wiki/Peter_Lawford "Peter Lawford") guest stars. **NOTE:** First appearance of the character Millicent Shand. | | | | | | |
| 239 | 22 | "My Friend, the Enemy" | Noel Black | Gerry Day | April 13, 1978 (1978-04-13) | 1310-1729-0721 |
| A reporter ([Luciana Paluzzi](/wiki/Luciana_Paluzzi "Luciana Paluzzi")) from an Italian magazine unearths a plot to kidnap a visiting Middle Eastern princess. (Similar to episode "The Cop on The Cover") | | | | | | |
| 240 | 23 | "A Stranger in His Grave" | Richard Benedict | Arthur Bernard Lewis | April 27, 1978 (1978-04-27) | 1310-1729-0722 |
| After the body of a wealthy rancher is found in a burning cane field, his widow becomes a prime suspect. [Andrew Prine](/wiki/Andrew_Prine "Andrew Prine"), [Laraine Stephens](/wiki/Laraine_Stephens "Laraine Stephens"), [Ted Markland](/wiki/Ted_Markland "Ted Markland") and [John Hillerman](/wiki/John_Hillerman "John Hillerman") guest stars. **NOTE:** This episode marks the final appearance of recurring character Jonathan Kaye (here played by [Lyle Bettger](/wiki/Lyle_Bettger "Lyle Bettger")). | | | | | | |
| 241 | 24 | "A Death in the Family" | Don Weis | Robert Janes | May 4, 1978 (1978-05-04) | 1310-1729-0723 |
| When the Five-O team suffers a devastating loss with its close-knit ranks, an enraged McGarrett asks a Hawaiian organized crime boss for help. [Reni Santoni](/wiki/Reni_Santoni "Reni Santoni") and [Manu Tupou](/wiki/Manu_Tupou "Manu Tupou") guest stars. | | | | | | |
